title: dysoxylum latifolium
intro: Dysoxylum latifolium, also known as the Kani or the Malabar kani, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dysoxylum latifolium include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Dysoxylum latifolium include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
